concept: But also (correlative closer) / Completing the not-only pair
description: The closing member of the correlative pair 'whera...whero' (not only...but also). Announces the second element that expands beyond the first, delivering the addition that 'whera' promised. Completes the revelation that there is more than initially presented.
sound_symbolism: Shares the breathy 'wh' onset with 'whera', maintaining the acoustic signature of revelation. The shift from 'a' to 'o' delivers the promised expansion, the rounded 'o' opening into fullness where 'a' merely anticipated.
grammatical_notes: Functions as the second member of the correlative conjunction pair 'whera...whero'. Appears before the second (additional) element. Must always be paired with a preceding 'whera'. The complete construction is: 'whera [element 1] whero [element 2]'.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The closing correlative delivers on the promise of expansion, completing a construction that adds without diminishing. Both elements are affirmed, supporting communication patterns that build rather than tear down.
pillars.solarpunk-values: The completion of the expansive pair embodies the solarpunk value that solutions and possibilities are richer than they first appear. Discovery and addition are celebrated.
